gid_chunky_mix.fertilizer_notes: Low nutrient retention due to chunky composition. Feed at half strength every 2-3 weeks during growing season. Bark and husk break down slowly adding minimal organic nutrition over time.

leca_pebbles.fertilizer_notes: LECA holds zero nutrients. Fertilizer must be added directly to reservoir water at quarter strength with every water change. Use a hydroponic nutrient solution for best results.

peat_moss.fertilizer_notes: Acidic pH can lock out nutrients even when present. Use a balanced fertilizer with micronutrients at half strength monthly. Consider pH adjustment if plant shows deficiency symptoms.

sphagnum_moss.water_frequency_modifier: monitor closely — holds moisture but dries fast on surface
sphagnum_moss.fertilizer_notes: Very low inherent nutrients. Feed at quarter to half strength every 2-3 weeks during active growth. Sphagnum can absorb and hold fertilizer salts so flush thoroughly every few months.

succulent_mix.fertilizer_notes: Minimal nutrient retention by design. Feed sparingly at quarter strength once monthly during growing season only. Never fertilize during dormancy. Over-fertilizing succulents causes etiolation and root burn.

minerals.fertilizer_notes: Inorganic mineral media holds virtually no nutrients. Regular feeding required at half strength every 2 weeks during growing season. Zeolite if present can hold some cation nutrients and release slowly.

water.fertilizer_notes: Full hydroponic setup — all nutrition must come from nutrient solution. Use a complete hydroponic fertilizer with macro and micronutrients at manufacturer recommended concentration. Monitor pH closely as it drifts in water. Change reservoir fully every 1-2 weeks to prevent salt buildup and pathogen growth.
avoid_for: root rot prone species without monitoring

coco_coir.water_frequency_modifier: moderate — dries more evenly than peat
coco_coir.fertilizer_notes: pH neutral and sustainable alternative to peat. Naturally low in nutrients so regular feeding required. Feed at half strength every 2 weeks during growing season. Coir can bind calcium and magnesium so consider a Cal-Mag supplement especially under LED lighting.

potting_soil.water_frequency_modifier: standard — allow top inch to dry between waterings
potting_soil.fertilizer_notes: Most commercial potting soils include a fertilizer charge lasting 3-6 months. Do not fertilize newly potted plants for the first 3 months. After charge depletes feed at half strength monthly during growing season. Watch for salt buildup — flush t
